headline: Big consulting firms betting billions on AI—and it's reshaping how deals get done

stories.title: PE Firms Replace $500K McKinsey Reports With $50K AI Agents
stories.summary: DiligenceSquared, a startup founded by a ex-BCG consultant, just convinced major PE firms managing $2+ trillion in combined assets to replace traditional consulting due diligence with AI agents. Thing is, five months of ops isn't enough data to know if this works at scale.
stories.takeaway: The old consulting playbook is broken. Cost just collapsed 90%, which kills the info asymmetry that made boutique PE shops dangerous.

stories.title: OpenAI Inks Multiyear Deals With McKinsey, BCG, Accenture & Capgemini
stories.summary: OpenAI just formally locked in consulting partnerships to help enterprises actually deploy its Frontier platform into production. These deals signal OpenAI's betting on big firms as distribution partners to move AI from pilot limbo to real workflows.
stories.takeaway: Consultants are becoming AI sales reps. The firms helping enterprises go live with OpenAI tech will own the enterprise AI deployment market.

stories.title: Accenture Jumped 8% After Announcing Mistral AI Deal & Faculty Acquisition
stories.summary: Accenture's betting big on 'Physical AI'—embedding AI into robotics and infrastructure—by partnering with Mistral and acquiring Faculty's 400+ specialists. Market's saying this is the move to pivot from cost anxiety to growth play.
stories.takeaway: Consulting's AI playbook is shifting from generative AI chatbots to agentic systems that control actual physical systems. That's where the real money flows.

watchNext.title: Deloitte Unveils Enterprise AI Navigator Platform
watchNext.why: Deloitte's moving AI from expense to strategic asset with a tool designed to shift thinking from 'can we do AI' to 'where should we deploy it.' Worth watching how the Big Four tools compete for real market share vs just PR.

watchNext.title: McKinsey's AI Agent Fleet Hit 20,000—Growing 500% in 18 Months
watchNext.why: Tbh this is lowkey terrifying for junior consultants. If McKinsey's internal AI agents are handling 80% of analyst work, the junior consultant pipeline just got way smaller. This is the real story nobody's talking about.
